internal_issues.description: Wrong author citation: detail_evidence cited Raghuraman et al. 2025 at doi:10.1073/pnas.2511595122, but CrossRef+OpenAlex confirm paper is Loeb, Thorsen, Kato, Rose, Hodnebrog 2025. A dome defender clicking the link sees Loeb on the masthead.
internal_issues.fix_details: Replaced 'Raghuraman et al., 2025, PNAS' with 'Loeb et al., 2025, PNAS' in detail_evidence via self-applied patch.

internal_issues.description: WIN-060 post-hoc paragraph led with monitor.py-absence as the disqualifier, allowing the circularity argument ('you penalize us for not monitoring'). EXP-295 restructured it to lead with the 390-year public-observable claim.
internal_issues.fix_details: Applied EXP-295-P1 from analyst expansion. Post-hoc paragraph now leads with the Halley 1701 / 390-year public-observable argument; monitor.py absence demoted to symptom.

internal_issues.description: Verbatim duplication: 0.0466 deg/yr unverifiable sentence appeared identically in paragraph 1 and again as final sentence of post-hoc paragraph.
internal_issues.fix_details: Removed duplicate trailing sentence from detail_verdict_text.

internal_issues.description: detail_verdict_text asserted 'no numerical ratio, no time period, and no derivation' — factually wrong since dome specifies 3:1. Inconsistent with already-fixed detail_evidence and TLDRs.
internal_issues.fix_details: Replaced 'no numerical ratio, no time period, and no derivation' framing with accurate 'specifies 3:1 but adopts it from CHAOS-7; no geometrically-derived prediction' framing.

internal_issues.description: W019 (dome's own weekly directional test for NMP) marked REFINED after lateral drift was 5.5× larger than poleward — directly contradicts WIN-043's claim. detail_extra is null; missing argument.
Gaia DR3 label but EDR3 URL (Riello 2021 is EDR3 photometric calibration paper, not DR3 release). Citation mismatch persisting from c2.
Polaris 1,260 L☉ at 433 ly + Sigma Octantis 40 L☉ at 294 ly gives Δm≈2.91 via F∝L/d², but observed Δm≈3.44. Internal inconsistency; Polaris L likely closer to 2,000-2,500 L☉.
TLDR says dome geometry can't produce hemispheric asymmetry; detail shows dome over-predicts by 250-800×. Strongest argument buried — TLDR should lead with the over-prediction magnitude.

recommended_actions.action: ANALYST: Add W019 REFINED/FALSIFIED status to WIN-043.detail_extra (ISS-1902). Dome's own weekly prediction W019 was 'poleward NMP drift dominates lateral' — observed lateral drift 5.5× larger; dome marked REFINED. This is direct dome self-contradiction and strengthens WIN-043 significantly. Curmudgeon WIN-043.c3 hole #1 has exact wording template.

Fix WIN-033 Gaia citation mismatch (ISS-1904): URL points to Riello 2021 (EDR3 photometric calibration) but label says 'Gaia DR3'. Either update URL to doi:10.1051/0004-6361/202243940 (actual DR3 release) or change label to 'Gaia EDR3'. Also fix Polaris luminosity inconsistency (ISS-1905): 1,260 L☉ → ~2,400 L☉ to reconcile the implied Δm with observed.

Fix WIN-066 TLDR tension (ISS-1906): tldr_evidence currently says dome 'can't produce hemisphere-dependent asymmetry' but detail_evidence shows dome over-predicts by 250-800×. Rewrite TLDR to lead with the 250-800× over-prediction magnitude — that is the strongest argument and should be visible without expanding.
